data.name: Adagio Holdings
data.howToRaise: Run Adagio salvage contracts: small-fee 5,000 aUEC missions (+100 rep), standard 10,000 aUEC missions (+150 rep), and turn-in missions (~+150 rep). Each contract before Senior also costs 10K-20K aUEC in fees. The intro 'Adagio Holdings in Need of Salvagers' is a rank-0, once-only mission (+100 rep, 48-min cooldown, any salvage ship).
data.unlocks: Tiered salvage contracts. Senior Contractor unlocks the 100,000 aUEC-fee missions (+200 rep each) that spawn large-hull wrecks: C2, M2, A2 Hercules and 890 Jump - the source of the Titanium cargo farm and bulk RMC. Higher tiers (Veteran, Head Contractor, Elite Contractor) may later gate blueprints/modules/crafting.
data.opposedBy: Mostly self-contained; the trade-off is the heavy aUEC fee sink, not a rival faction. Criminal salvage (e.g. unlawful claim-jumping) can sour lawful standing.

data.name: Wicko (Hathor OLP event giver)
data.howToRaise: Complete the Hathor Orbital Laser Platform mission chain: align PAF satellites, power and fire the OLP, and mine carite from the opened caves. Each completed site/objective grants favor.
data.unlocks: Armor sets, Volt-series energy weapons (Volt Parallax rifle, Volt SMG), and exclusive ship rewards earned individually through accumulated favor.
data.opposedBy: none documented

data.name: Covalex Shipping
data.howToRaise: Complete cargo-hauling contracts. Start with the 'Opportunity for Independent Cargo Hauler' intro contract. Fastest method is contract stacking out of Everus Harbor (Hurston) — deliver multiple overlapping contracts to the same destination. Tiers seen: Rookie -> Junior (~4 contracts) -> Member (stacking up to 16) -> Experienced (~26 missions) -> Senior/Master.
data.unlocks: Rank ladder Trainee > Rookie > Junior > Member > Experienced > Senior > Master. Member: hold up to 16 active contracts. Experienced: large Hull-C-class bulk jobs. Master: interstellar hauling missions + military quantum-drive blueprint progression (VK-00, XL-1, TS-2). Higher ranks hide lower-tier contracts from the list.
data.opposedBy: Largely non-conflicting with other corporates, but favoring one hauling contractor over another (Covalex vs Red Wind Linehaul vs Ling Family Hauling) limits cross-access; criminal smuggling lowers lawful corporate standing.

data.name: Red Wind Linehaul
data.howToRaise: Complete the 'Red Wind Seeking New Haulers' intro contract, then grind Red Wind freight in Pyro. Stack same-destination contracts (Gaslight to Ruin Station at Experienced; ~10-contract stacks at Senior).
data.unlocks: Company ladder with longer routes, larger loads and far higher payouts than Stanton lawful hauling. Experienced surfaces ~795K aUEC stackable contracts; Senior is the patch's top hauling income (7-9M aUEC/hour, 11M+ per ~90 min run).
data.opposedBy: Choosing one hauling employer over another affects access; criminal cargo activity lowers lawful standing.

data.name: Ling Family Hauling
data.howToRaise: Complete 'Cargo Hauling Opportunity with Ling Hauling' to become a 'trusted contractor', then keep completing Ling freight around Crusader. Level it on its own track.
data.unlocks: Standard hauling ladder PLUS stealth-component rewards: Zephyr stealth quantum drive, Eclipse stealth power plant, Spectral stealth option - valuable for stealth ship builds and crafting.
data.opposedBy: Cross-contractor access trade-off; criminal activity reduces lawful standing.

data.name: Dead Saints
data.howToRaise: Accept 'Rookie Haul' / 'Trial Cargo Haul' from the Unverified contract tab, then run smuggling contracts (cryopods, experimental pharma, dirty cargo). Carrying illegal cargo in monitored space generates CrimeStat.
data.unlocks: Access to higher-paying smuggling/cryopod contracts. Headline run: ~386K aUEC per cryopod mission, ~3.86M aUEC for a 10-mission stack delivered to Sunset Mesa (Pyro II) in 30-40 min.
data.opposedBy: Lawful authorities (Crusader Security / Hurston / microTech) - illegal cargo triggers CrimeStat and interdiction in lawful systems.

data.name: Bounty Hunters Guild
data.howToRaise: Complete bounty contracts of increasing risk tier (VLRT, LRT, MRT, HRT, VHRT, ERT). Each higher-difficulty kill grants more rep; ERT gives the most rep and ~100k aUEC. Reaching a rep threshold triggers a certification mission whose completion grants the next license. ~7 progression levels total.
data.unlocks: Tracker certification ladder: Tracker Training Permit (VLRT) > Beginner's Permit (LRT) > Tracker License (MRT) > Journeyman (HRT) > Advanced (VHRT) > Master Tracker License (ERT). Plus PvP certs (Fugitive Recovery, Suspect Apprehension). Pay bonuses at higher Guild ranks (Probationary > Junior > Member > Senior > Veteran > Guild Steward).
data.opposedBy: Criminal/outlaw factions (Vaughn, Nine Tails, Headhunters). Gaining a CrimeStat or completing criminal contracts lowers Guild and security standing; the Guild may refuse to employ players with active criminal reputation.

data.name: Crusader Security
data.howToRaise: Take bounty and security contracts in Crusader space (Stanton). Overseen by the Bounty Hunters Guild — completing its bounties raises both Crusader Security and Guild standing in parallel.
data.unlocks: Crusader-jurisdiction bounties (up to your Guild cert tier) and the mercenary contract ladder leading to UGF Alerts. Best starting system for combined merc + bounty grinding.
data.opposedBy: Outlaw factions and CrimeStat. Killing lawful NPCs or running criminal contracts reduces standing.

data.name: Hurston Security
data.howToRaise: Complete bounty and security/patrol contracts issued in the Hurston system. Also overseen by the Bounty Hunters Guild, so rep gains feed the Guild ladder.
data.unlocks: Hurston-region bounties by tier, higher payouts, and access/clearance within Hurston Dynamics jurisdiction.
data.opposedBy: Outlaw factions, CrimeStat in Hurston space.

data.name: InterSec Defense Solutions
data.howToRaise: Complete the InterSec intro mission in Nyx, then run InterSec Defense Solutions contracts repeatedly until Rank III. Rep was reset by the 4.8 wipe.
data.unlocks: Tactical Strike Group (TSG) operation at Rank III (mobiGlas > Verified Contracts > Mercenary). TSG rewards include Novia Crossbow blueprints, FPS/component blueprints, Grade-A components, unique bomber jacket.
data.opposedBy: Shattered Blade, Vanduul

data.name: Mercenary (Crusader Security / multi-org)
data.howToRaise: Clear security/bunker contracts (start with Security Work Assessment), then defend-site missions, rotating microTech/Hurston/ArcCorp/Crusader to dodge cooldowns. UGF Alerts give the biggest rep boosts.
data.unlocks: UGF Alerts and the final tiers of mercenary contracts; Gilly's Combat Gauntlet scenario chain is the flagship money loop on this track.
data.opposedBy: Outlaw / criminal NPC factions

data.name: Northrock Service Group
data.howToRaise: Complete bounty contracts issued by Northrock; one of the three security orgs under the Bounty Hunters Guild umbrella.
data.unlocks: Northrock bounty contracts and associated Guild rep progression.
data.opposedBy: Outlaw factions and CrimeStat.

data.name: Vaughn
data.howToRaise: Complete Vaughn criminal contracts. Early ones are rough; chaining 'Complicated Task' (the money tier — fast refresh, solid rep, fighter-killable targets) accelerates the curve. 'All Things Come To An End' is the key blueprint/loot mission.
data.unlocks: Criminal contracts tied to weapon/armor blueprints (Pulverizer LMG, Tripledown Pistol, Killshot Rifle, Deadrig Shotgun), rare armor (Citadel, Carnifex, Piecemeal, Fortifier/Badami helmets), 8x low-light scopes, and better assassination missions.
data.opposedBy: Bounty Hunters Guild, Crusader/Hurston Security and other lawful factions — raising Vaughn rep and running criminal contracts incurs CrimeStat and lowers lawful standing. This is the core lawful-vs-criminal trade-off.

data.name: Nine Tails
data.howToRaise: N/A — antagonist faction; fought during Siege of Orison and at sites like Whistler's Crypt.
data.unlocks: Black-market missions, pirate sanctuary access, contraband discounts, faction-specific rewards.
data.opposedBy: Lawful factions (UEE, security forces, Bounty Hunters Guild) and Citizens for Prosperity. Gaining pirate approval turns lawful and pro-citizen factions hostile.

data.name: Headhunters (Pyro)
data.howToRaise: Side with this Pyro faction via its missions; an early allegiance choice in Pyro locks your faction path.
data.unlocks: Pyro outlaw contracts, sanctuary access and faction rewards aligned to that side.
data.opposedBy: Opposing Pyro factions — the early choice locks allegiance and steers all subsequent missions/rewards; siding here turns the rival Pyro faction hostile.

data.name: Mining / Refinery Guild
data.howToRaise: Complete mining missions/contracts. Reputation rises within the mining guild as contracts are completed. In 4.8 mined material uses 8 static quality bands — bank Band 5-8 for best returns.
data.unlocks: Better-paying mining contracts and more profession gear. Planned perks: reduced ship expedite fees, refinery shop discounts, faster refining times, priority refinery equipment use, loyalty bonuses on ore sales. Refineries at HUR-L1, ARC-L1, MIC-L1.
data.opposedBy: Largely non-conflicting; some mining orgs (e.g. Shubin Mining) still lack full mission/reputation tracks as of 4.8.

data.name: Medical / Search-and-Rescue
data.howToRaise: Perform medical rescues — respond to player incapacitation beacons, revive/stabilize and transport to a hospital (~50k aUEC per rescue). Medical careers are slated to gain their own reputation line like bounty hunting.
data.unlocks: Per-rescue payouts now; a dedicated medical reputation track with rescue-role progression is still being built out in 4.x.
data.opposedBy: Non-conflicting support role; no direct rival faction.

data.name: Civilian Defence Force (CDF)
data.howToRaise: Participate in CDF-coordinated server events — accept the CDF activation contract in mobiGlas, then complete XenoThreat phases and Siege of Orison objectives. Consistency across event runs builds rank.
data.unlocks: Event payouts and account-bound flair including the XenoThreat armor set (ORC-mkX helmet/core/arms/legs, Modified). Higher rank gates the full armor set.
data.opposedBy: XenoThreat, Nine Tails

data.name: Ruto / Criminal Network
data.howToRaise: Take the unlawful path in events (accept Jumptown from Ruto with any CrimeStat) and sell contraband at Grim HEX.
data.unlocks: Unlawful Jumptown contraband missions and criminal-side payouts.
data.opposedBy: Crusader Security, UEE

data.name: Foxwell Enforcement
data.howToRaise: Accept and complete lawful Defend Location asteroid-base defense missions (4.8.1).
data.unlocks: Higher-tier Defend Location missions and component-blueprint rewards on the lawful side.
data.opposedBy: Headhunters

data.name: Bit Zeros
data.howToRaise: Complete Data Heist missions at microTech Underground Facilities. Each completed heist raises standing.
data.unlocks: Higher-difficulty, higher-reward Data Heist variants (riskier servers/objectives, larger aUEC payouts ~30k → 60k+).
data.opposedBy: UGF site owners / facility security

data.name: Wildstar Racing
data.howToRaise: Complete PU time-trial missions on Wildstar tracks; better finishing times and repeated runs raise reputation.
data.unlocks: Additional time-trial tracks and larger cumulative aUEC rewards at higher rep tiers.

data.name: BlacJac Security
data.type: Private security / mercenary contractor (Stanton, ArcCorp area)
data.howToRaise: Complete BlacJac security and contraband missions such as Seize & Destroy Narcotics / Eliminate Drug Stash.
data.unlocks: Further security and counter-narcotics contracts; standing within the ArcCorp security ecosystem.
data.opposedBy: Drug runners / outlaw factions whose stashes you raid

data.name: Citizens for Prosperity / Headhunters / Frontier Fighters
data.howToRaise: Pick one faction during the Fight for Pyro event and complete its mission line toward the faction's goal.
data.unlocks: Faction-specific event rewards (e.g. Pyro laser repeaters) and narrative outcomes; mutually exclusive allegiance for the event.
data.opposedBy: The two factions you did not choose
